title: Crispy Hunan Eggplant and Tofu
intro: Hunan cooking is known for its bold heat, sharp aromatics, and deep savory structure. This dish leans into that profile with crisped eggplant and tofu, layered with garlic, chili, and a balanced sauce that clings without becoming heavy. The goal is contrast: soft interior, crisp exterior, and a punchy, clean finish.

ingredientGroups.title: Tofu
items: 14 oz firm tofu, pressed and cut into 1-inch cubes

ingredientGroups.title: Eggplant
items: 1 large Chinese eggplant, cut into 1-inch chunks

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Tofu
items: Toss the tofu with cornstarch and salt until lightly coated.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat with oil and cook the tofu until golden and crisp on all sides. Remove and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Eggplant
items: Toss the eggplant with cornstarch and salt.
In the same pan, add oil as needed and cook the eggplant until browned on the outside and soft inside. Remove and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the Aromatics
items: Add oil to the pan and cook garlic, ginger, scallions, and dried chilies until fragrant, about 30 seconds.

stepGroups.title: Combine and Sauce
items: Return tofu and eggplant to the pan.
Stir the sauce to recombine and pour into the pan.
Cook, tossing continuously, until the sauce thickens and coats everything evenly.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Turn off heat and add sesame oil and cilantro.
